data.title: Zenith Minerals Director Exercises Options, Boosts Shareholding
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

The latest announcement is out from Zenith Minerals Limited ( (AU:ZNC) ).

Zenith Minerals Limited has reported a change in director Euan Jenkins’ holdings, reflecting the exercise of previously held options into ordinary shares. Following the transaction, Jenkins’ stake increased to 16,938,441 ordinary shares, while his existing Class A to Class E performance rights remain unchanged.

The adjustment in Jenkins’ equity position consolidates his interest into fully paid shares, removing a tranche of options from the company’s capital structure. This shift modestly alters the director’s exposure profile and may be seen as a signal of confidence in Zenith Minerals’ prospects by converting derivative securities into direct share ownership.

More about Zenith Minerals Limited

data.description:

Horizon Oil Limited ( (AU:HZN) ) has issued an update.

Horizon Oil Limited has completed its off‑market takeover offer for Cue Energy Resources, with the bid closing on 19 June 2026. Following the offer, the company has released an updated distribution schedule of shareholders and a list of its 20 largest holders, outlining the concentration of ownership.

The disclosure shows that the vast majority of Horizon’s issued capital is held by investors with stakes above 100,001 shares, and that a small number of institutional and nominee entities control significant positions. This ownership profile underscores a high degree of shareholder concentration, which may influence future governance dynamics and liquidity in the company’s shares.

More about Horizon Oil Limited

Horizon Oil Limited is an Australian oil and gas company listed on the ASX, focused on exploration and production activities. The company participates in regional energy markets through interests in upstream petroleum assets and related investments.

An update from Forrestania Resources Limited ( (AU:FRS) ) is now available.

Forrestania Resources Ltd has confirmed that 518,921 of its listed options, trading under ASX code FRSOA, have lapsed following their scheduled expiry on 30 June 2026. The lapse of these options slightly simplifies the company’s capital structure by reducing the volume of outstanding derivative securities tied to its ordinary shares.

The company had previously notified registered option holders of the impending expiry in May 2026, and the process has now concluded in line with the terms of issue. While the announcement does not indicate any immediate operational changes, the removal of these options may have minor implications for future potential share dilution and could be relevant for existing shareholders assessing capital management.

Candy Club Holdings Ltd. ( (AU:SCP) ) has provided an update.

Scalare Partners Holdings Ltd. has announced a security consolidation affecting its ordinary fully paid shares, restricted ordinary shares, and listed options, with the change subject to shareholder approval on 4 August 2026. The consolidation, which encompasses both equity and option classes expiring in 2027, is intended to streamline the company’s register and potentially enhance market perceptions of its securities. data.title: Minrex strengthens Serbian leadership team for Tlamino Gold Project
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

MinRex Resources Limited ( (AU:MRR) ) just unveiled an update.

Minrex Resources has strengthened its Serbian operations by appointing geologist Dr Milorad Antić as Exploration Manager and mining lawyer Marko Ćurić as General Counsel. The new hires bring deep regional exploration, legal and regulatory expertise as the company progresses drilling at the Barje Deposit within the Tlamino Gold Project.

Antić previously worked on Tlamino in its early stages and has extensive experience across Serbia’s Serbo-Macedonian Massif, while Ćurić has advised numerous Balkan resource companies and helped shape regional mining laws. Management says the appointments give Minrex one of the strongest in-country teams in the Balkans, positioning the company to navigate permitting, government relations and technical challenges as it seeks to unlock Tlamino’s value for shareholders.

More about MinRex Resources Limited

Minrex Resources Limited is an Australian-based, ASX-listed gold and copper explorer with advanced exploration and development assets across Serbia and Australia. The company’s flagship asset is the Tlamino Gold Project in southern Serbia, where it is advancing drilling at the Barje Deposit and building in-country capability to support project development.

Average Trading Volume: 1,574,327

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$29.94M

data.title: Greenbrier Companies Earnings Call Shows Resilient Leasing
data.description:
Greenbrier Companies ((GBX)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Greenbrier Companies’ latest earnings call struck a cautiously upbeat tone, underscoring a resilient, well-capitalized business despite visible industry headwinds. Management highlighted stronger margins, highly utilized leased assets, and robust liquidity as key cushions against softer new-build demand, regulatory uncertainty, and timing shifts that push some activity into fiscal 2027.

Quarterly Revenue and Profitability

Greenbrier reported quarterly revenue of $577 million, with aggregate gross margin improving sequentially to 14.1%, now within its long-term target range. Earnings from operations reached $32 million, about 6% of revenue, while EBITDA was $69 million, roughly 12% of revenue, producing diluted EPS of $0.60.

Leasing and Fleet Management Expansion

Leasing remains a bright spot as the owned lease fleet expanded to 20,600 railcars with utilization at a standout 99%. Leasing and fleet revenue rose to $47 million, up 3% from the prior quarter, supported by lease originations that represented 60% of total global orders and about 4,400 secondary market railcar acquisitions.

Order Intake and Backlog

Order intake for the quarter reached 2,200 railcars valued at $340 million, signaling continued customer demand despite macro uncertainty. Greenbrier’s backlog closed at 13,800 railcars worth $2.0 billion, providing solid visibility for future production and revenue and helping to smooth through cyclical swings.

Strong Balance Sheet and Liquidity

The company emphasized its strong balance sheet, with total liquidity around $887 million, including $274 million in cash and $613 million of available borrowing capacity. Greenbrier deployed $227 million to grow its leased railcar portfolio and refinanced its leasing term loan with a new $300 million facility, extended for six years and supplemented with a $125 million delayed draw.

Capital Allocation and Shareholder Returns

Greenbrier continues to balance growth investments with cash returns to shareholders, declaring a quarterly dividend of $0.34 per share, its 49th consecutive payout. Management noted about $65 million remains under its share repurchase authorization and reiterated a disciplined approach with an annual lease-fleet investment target of up to $300 million.

Operational and Commercial Execution

The manufacturing segment showed improved operating efficiency and margin progression even at low production levels, aided by insourcing investments that support cost control. Wheelset shipments exceeded internal expectations, and the Brazil joint venture, Greenbrier-Maxion, delivered strong operational and financial performance, driven largely by agriculture and biodiesel demand.

Guidance Narrowing and Outlook

Greenbrier maintained its fiscal 2026 revenue outlook at $2.4 billion to $2.5 billion but narrowed its EPS guidance to a tighter $3.00 to $3.15 range. Management said the refinement reflects increased visibility and confidence while acknowledging that fourth-quarter manufacturing margins and delivery timing, including activity shifting into fiscal 2027, could pressure near-term results.

Commercial Flexibility and Market Position

Executives highlighted the company’s integrated go-to-market model as a core competitive advantage, spanning direct sales, leasing partnerships, and syndication. This commercial flexibility allows Greenbrier to steer more volume into leasing, manage production levels more effectively, and support high utilization across regions even as demand patterns change.

Weak Near-Term New Car Demand

Industry data points to a tougher backdrop for new railcar builds, particularly in North America, where forecasts project fewer than 25,000 new cars in 2026, the lowest since 2010. That compares with roughly 35,000 average annual deliveries since 2020, and this decline is already weighing on new-build demand and manufacturing revenue.

Sequential Manufacturing Revenue Decline

Manufacturing revenue slipped about 2% sequentially to $529 million, primarily due to fewer new railcar deliveries in the quarter. This shortfall was partly offset by higher maintenance program revenue, but the trend underscores how closely Greenbrier’s top line is tied to industry build rates.

Timing Shift of Activity into Fiscal 2027

Management noted that some manufacturing and delivery activity has moved out of the current fiscal year and into fiscal 2027. These timing shifts are a key reason for the narrower EPS range and suggest potential pressure on fourth-quarter manufacturing margins as certain projects slide into the next period.

Regulatory and Trade Uncertainty

Executives flagged ongoing uncertainty from trade and regulatory developments, including Section 301 amendments that could affect equipment moving from Mexico to the U.S. and coupler-related determinations under review. While customer passthroughs may mitigate eventual costs, unclear retroactive tariff obligations are adding industry-wide ambiguity.

Muted Europe Demand and Footprint Actions

European wagon demand remains subdued, with about 9,000 units expected for calendar 2026, prompting Greenbrier to tighten its footprint. Management completed facility consolidation, focusing on streamlined production and inventory reduction, but weaker regional demand is weighing on near-term European performance.

Gains on Sale Expected to Moderate

Earlier periods benefited from substantial gains on sale of assets, which helped smooth earnings volatility and boosted fee income. Management now expects these gains to be modest in the fourth quarter and likely to wind down over time, removing a small cushion from future reported results.

Forward-Looking Guidance and Expectations

Looking ahead, Greenbrier’s guidance suggests steady, though not explosive, growth, with fiscal 2026 revenue of $2.4 billion to $2.5 billion and EPS of $3.00 to $3.15. The company is leaning on its high-utilization lease fleet, sizable backlog, and strong liquidity to navigate soft new-build demand and regulatory clouds as some manufacturing activity rolls into fiscal 2027.

Greenbrier’s earnings call painted the picture of a company leaning on leasing strength and balance-sheet resilience to offset cyclical and regulatory challenges. For investors, the key takeaway is a business that is managing margins and capital allocation carefully, with visible production booked ahead, even as the industry braces for its lowest new-car build year in more than a decade.
